instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: There are several parts of a class, much like there are several parts of an object

text_blocks.text: A good rule of thumb: Restrict everything as much as possible.  If a variable does not need to change, make it final to prevent possible accidents.  If a variable does not need to be directly visible to everyone, make it private.

To reference non-static object-dependent variables, use the keyword this followed by the dot operator.

text_blocks.text: The keyword can distinguish these object-dependent variables and parameters.

text_blocks.text: It can be used regularly, with no need to declare them.

text_blocks.text: Should we allow the function caller (e.g. random user) to make the car’s ID number?  Probably not.

runs.text: Should we allow the function caller (e.g. random user) to make the car’s ID number?  Probably not.

text_blocks.text: We want the ID number to be unique.

runs.text: We want the ID number to be unique.

text_blocks.text: Simple solution: just count up by one every time a new car is made.

runs.text: Simple solution: just count up by one every time a new car is made.

text_blocks.text: We can do this via a class-level variable.

runs.text: We can do this via a class-level variable.

text_blocks.text: Because nextIDNum is static, and was initialized to 0, the first car has an ID number of 0.  Then the static value is increased by 1.  The next time we construct a new car, its ID number will be 1.

runs.text: is static, and was initialized to 0, the first car has an ID number of 0.  Then the static value is increased by 1.  The next time we construct a new car, its ID number will be 1.

text_blocks.text: The most common functions are getters and setters
Getters retrieve the values of instance variables and return them

runs.text: Getters retrieve the values of instance variables and return them

text_blocks.text: Setters alter the values of the instance variables

text_blocks.text: Why use these functions rather than setting the variable to be public?

text_blocks.text: Having these methods ensure you have full control over the instance variables

text_blocks.text: Should you decide to change how the variable is accessed or altered, you only need to change the method.

runs.text: Should you decide to change how the variable is accessed or altered, you only need to change the method.
